message.content: ❯ Websearch how well gemma 4 runs on an NVIDIA 5090?  To be clear, I want to see if it runs well on this computer, which has other features too.

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.query: Gemma 4 NVIDIA RTX 5090 performance benchmark tokens per second 2026

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Gemma 4 NVIDIA RTX 5090 performance benchmark tokens per second 2026"

Links: [{"title":"RTX 5090 Performance on Ubuntu Gemma 3 · Issue #9701 · ollama/ollama","url":"https://github.com/ollama/ollama/issues/9701"},{"title":"Gemma 4 Speed Benchmark: Performance Guide 2026 - Gemma 4 Wiki","url":"https://www.gemma4.wiki/benchmark/gemma-4-speed-benchmark"},{"title":"LilaRest/gemma-4-31B-it-NVFP4-turbo · Hugging Face","url":"https://huggingface.co/LilaRest/gemma-4-31B-it-NVFP4-turbo"},{"title":"Deploy Google Gemma 4 on GPU Cloud: MoE and Dense Model Guide (2026) | Spheron Blog","url":"https://www.spheron.network/blog/deploy-gemma-4-gpu-cloud/"},{"title":"From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI","url":"https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/rtx-ai-garage-open-models-google-gemma-4/"},{"title":"Gemma 4 on RTX 3090 vs 4090 vs 5090 vs Mac: Benchmarks That Will Surprise You (31B and 26B-A4B) - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VYc47oqBnqI"},{"title":"Gemma 4 Day-1 Inference on NVIDIA DGX Spark — Preliminary Benchmarks - DGX Spark / GB10 - NVIDIA Developer Forums","url":"https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/gemma-4-day-1-inference-on-nvidia-dgx-spark-preliminary-benchmarks/365503"},{"title":"Google Gemma 4 Developer Guide: Benchmarks & Local Setup | Lushbinary","url":"https://lushbinary.com/blog/gemma-4-developer-guide-benchmarks-architecture-local-deployment-2026/"},{"title":"What 415 Tokens Per Second Looks Like on a $4,000 GPU Rig | BDK Studios","url":"https://www.bdkstudios.com/blog/local-ai-inference-rtx-5090-benchmark"},{"title":"Gemma 4 LoRA Fine-Tuning on RTX 5090: What Works and What Doesn't","url":"https://ai.rs/ai-developer/gemma-4-lora-fine-tuning-rtx-5090"}]

Based on the search results, here are the key Gemma 4 performance benchmarks for the NVIDIA RTX 5090:

## Gemma 4 RTX 5090 Performance Benchmarks (2026)

**31B Dense Model:**
- Over 64 tokens/sec on the 31B model
- 65 tok/sec reflects full 31B computation per pass
- Prompt throughput of 1,174 tok/s and generation speed of 61.6 tok/s

**26B-A4B MoE (Mixture-of-Experts) Model:**
- 182+ t/s speed on an RTX 5090 for agentic workflows
- 120 tok/sec figure reflects its low active parameter count per token

**Single-Request Inference:**
- Single-request decode is ~51 tok/s with TTFT under 70ms

**High-Throughput Classification:**
- Expect 14+ req/s on RTX 5090 for classification workloads (~1K input, ~10 output tokens)

These benchmarks demonstrate that the RTX 5090 provides significant performance improvements, particularly for the MoE variant due to its selective parameter activation, while the dense 31B model achieves solid throughput constrained by memory bandwidth requirements.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.query: Gemma 4 model size VRAM requirements RTX 5090 32GB local inference

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Gemma 4 model size VRAM requirements RTX 5090 32GB local inference"

Links: [{"title":"Can I run Gemma 4 31B on RTX 5090 (32GB)? VRAM Required: 21.0GB","url":"https://gpuforllm.com/models/gemma-4-31b-on-rtx-5090?quant=4.85&context=8192"},{"title":"Deploy Google Gemma 4 on GPU Cloud: MoE and Dense Model Guide (2026) | Spheron Blog","url":"https://www.spheron.network/blog/deploy-gemma-4-gpu-cloud/"},{"title":"Gemma 4 31b VRAM Requirements: Full GPU Benchmark Guide 2026 - Gemma 4 Wiki","url":"https://www.gemma4.wiki/requirements/gemma-4-31b-vram-requirements"},{"title":"Gemma 4 Hardware Requirements: RAM, VRAM, and Model Size Guide","url":"https://avenchat.com/blog/gemma-4-hardware-requirements"},{"title":"LilaRest/gemma-4-31B-it-NVFP4-turbo · Hugging Face","url":"https://huggingface.co/LilaRest/gemma-4-31B-it-NVFP4-turbo"},{"title":"Gemma 4 - How to Run Locally | Unsloth Documentation","url":"https://unsloth.ai/docs/models/gemma-4"},{"title":"Gemma 4 Hardware Guide — 2B to 31B VRAM Requirements | Compute Market","url":"https://www.compute-market.com/blog/gemma-4-local-hardware-guide-2026"},{"title":"Gemma 4 LoRA Fine-Tuning on RTX 5090: What Works and What Doesn't","url":"https://ai.rs/ai-developer/gemma-4-lora-fine-tuning-rtx-5090"},{"title":"Can Gemma 4 26B A4B run on RTX 5090 32GB?","url":"https://willitrunai.com/can-run/gemma-4-26b-a4b-on-rtx-5090-32gb"},{"title":"From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI","url":"https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/rtx-ai-garage-open-models-google-gemma-4/"}]

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about Gemma 4 model sizes and VRAM requirements for the RTX 5090 with 32GB VRAM:

## Gemma 4 Model Variants and VRAM Requirements

**Gemma 4 31B (Dense Model):**
- Running Gemma 4 31B on RTX 5090 (32GB) requires 21.0GB of VRAM to load the weights and maintain an 8,192 token context window
- The RTX 5090 with 32GB GDDR7 runs the 31B Dense at Q8 with room for long context windows
- For the 31B, the increase from 4K to 256K context is larger (20 GB at 4K → 40 GB at 256K)

**Gemma 4 26B-A4B (Mixture of Experts):**
- Gemma 4 26B A4B needs ~23.4 GB VRAM with Q4_K_M quantization
- The AWQ INT4 MoE variant requires roughly 15GB VRAM for model weights, and an RTX 5090 (32GB) runs it with room for larger batches

## Local Inference Performance

The RTX 5090 stands out as the clear winner in 2026, with 32GB of high-speed VRAM comfortably fitting a 4-bit or 5-bit version of the 31B model while leaving plenty of room for a large context window and system overhead

The RTX 5090 32GB can run Gemma 4 26B A4B well with an expected decode speed of 53.2 tok/s

## Key Compatibility Summary

- **31B Dense**: Fully compatible with RTX 5090 at multiple quantization levels (Q4-Q8)
- **26B MoE**: Comfortably runs with headroom for larger batches and higher context windows
- Both models are recommended for serious local inference on the RTX 5090


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about Gemma 4 model sizes and VRAM requirements for the RTX 5090 with 32GB VRAM:

## Gemma 4 Model Variants and VRAM Requirements

**Gemma 4 31B (Dense Model):**
- Running Gemma 4 31B on RTX 5090 (32GB) requires 21.0GB of VRAM to load the weights and maintain an 8,192 token context window
- The RTX 5090 with 32GB GDDR7 runs the 31B Dense at Q8 with room for long context windows
- For the 31B, the increase from 4K to 256K context is larger (20 GB at 4K → 40 GB at 256K)

**Gemma 4 26B-A4B (Mixture of Experts):**
- Gemma 4 26B A4B needs ~23.4 GB VRAM with Q4_K_M quantization
- The AWQ INT4 MoE variant requires roughly 15GB VRAM for model weights, and an RTX 5090 (32GB) runs it with room for larger batches

## Local Inference Performance

The RTX 5090 stands out as the clear winner in 2026, with 32GB of high-speed VRAM comfortably fitting a 4-bit or 5-bit version of the 31B model while leaving plenty of room for a large context window and system overhead

The RTX 5090 32GB can run Gemma 4 26B A4B well with an expected decode speed of 53.2 tok/s

## Key Compatibility Summary

- **31B Dense**: Fully compatible with RTX 5090 at multiple quantization levels (Q4-Q8)
- **26B MoE**: Comfortably runs with headroom for larger batches and higher context windows
- Both models are recommended for serious local inference on the RTX 5090

content.text:
Gemma 4 runs comfortably on an RTX 5090 — its 32GB GDDR7 is the right tier for this model family.

**What fits on a 5090 (32GB):**
- **Gemma 4 31B (dense)** at Q4–Q5 uses ~21 GB for weights with room for an 8K+ context. Q8 fits with shorter contexts.
- **Gemma 4 26B-A4B (MoE)** at Q4 needs ~15–23 GB, leaving headroom for larger batches/contexts.

**Throughput on a 5090:**
- 31B dense: ~60–65 tok/s generation, prompt processing ~1,170 tok/s.
- 26B-A4B MoE: ~120–180+ tok/s thanks to the small active-parameter count per token.
- Single-request decode TTFT under ~70 ms.

**Caveats for "this computer":** GPU VRAM is the dominant constraint, but for long contexts (>32K) on the 31B you'll start swapping into system RAM — DDR5 speed and PCIe 5.0 x16 matter then. CPU only matters for prompt preprocessing and tokenization. If you tell me the rest of the rig (RAM, CPU, NVMe), I can flag any bottlenecks.

Sources:
- [From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/rtx-ai-garage-open-models-google-gemma-4/)
- [Gemma 4 Speed Benchmark: Performance Guide 2026](https://www.gemma4.wiki/benchmark/gemma-4-speed-benchmark)
- [Gemma 4 on RTX 3090 vs 4090 vs 5090 vs Mac](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VYc47oqBnqI)
- [Can I run Gemma 4 31B on RTX 5090 (32GB)?](https://gpuforllm.com/models/gemma-4-31b-on-rtx-5090?quant=4.85&context=8192)
- [Gemma 4 31b VRAM Requirements](https://www.gemma4.wiki/requirements/gemma-4-31b-vram-requirements)
- [Gemma 4 LoRA Fine-Tuning on RTX 5090](https://ai.rs/ai-developer/gemma-4-lora-fine-tuning-rtx-5090)
- [What 415 Tokens Per Second Looks Like on a $4,000 GPU Rig](https://www.bdkstudios.com/blog/local-ai-inference-rtx-5090-benchmark)

lastPrompt: ❯ Websearch how well gemma 4 runs on an NVIDIA 5090?  To be clear, I want to see if it runs well on this computer, which has other features too.

message.content: Check the specs on my system using shell commands

content.text: I'll gather CPU, RAM, GPU, storage, and OS details in parallel.

input.command: free -h && echo "---" && sudo dmidecode -t memory 2>/dev/null | grep -E "Size:|Speed:|Type:|Manufacturer:" | head -30 || echo "dmidecode needs sudo"
lspci | grep -iE "pci.*bridge|nvme" | head -10 && echo "---" && sudo lshw -class display 2>/dev/null | head -20 || echo "lshw needs sudo"

content.text:
Here's the system breakdown for Gemma 4 readiness:

| Component | Spec | Verdict for Gemma 4 |
|-----------|------|---------------------|
| **GPU** | NVIDIA RTX 5090 (device `2b85`) | ✅ Hardware present — but **driver not loaded** (UNCLAIMED, `nvidia-smi` fails) |
| **CPU** | Intel Core Ultra 9 285K, 24 cores @ up to 5.1 GHz | ✅ Top-tier, no bottleneck |
| **RAM** | 188 GiB DDR5 (4× 48GB Kingston @ 4400 MT/s) | ✅ Huge — plenty for CPU offload of larger quants |
| **Storage** | 4× WD Black SN850X 2TB NVMe (PCIe 4.0/5.0), 5.5 TB mounted (md RAID), 4.8 TB free | ✅ Fast model loading |
| **OS** | Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS, kernel 6.8.0-111 | ✅ Supported |
| **Integrated GPU** | Intel Arrow Lake-U driving display @ 3840×2160 | Currently doing all display work |

**The blocker:** the RTX 5090 shows as `UNCLAIMED` — no NVIDIA driver bound to it. You'll need to install the NVIDIA driver (570+ series for Blackwell/5090) before any of those Gemma 4 throughput numbers apply. The iGPU is currently handling display, which is actually convenient — it leaves the 5090's full 32GB VRAM free for inference once driven.

**Minor note:** DDR5 reporting 4400 MT/s with all 4 slots populated is normal Intel behavior on Arrow Lake (4-DIMM topology de-rates from rated 5600+). Doesn't matter for GPU-resident inference; only matters if you spill the 31B's long-context KV cache to system RAM.

Want me to check for available NVIDIA driver packages, or look at how to install them?

content: You're checking whether Gemma 4 will run well on this machine; the RTX 5090 and CPU/RAM are more than sufficient, but the NVIDIA driver isn't loaded so the GPU is currently unusable. Next: install the NVIDIA 570+ driver for Blackwell. (disable recaps in /config)
